Motion To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY Motion To Set Aside Dismissal.

Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's motion pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 to set aside dismissal and enter judgment against Defendant Jose Owen Vargara Salazar, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Judgment shall be entered in favor of Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company and against Defendant Jose Owen Vargara Salazar in the principal amount of $3,434.56, plus reasonable costs of $300.50, for a total of $3,807.06.

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. J. TERRELLE BYNUM, Esq. of Law Offices of Brian P. Smith & Associates (Tel: 408-408-1759) appearing for defendant Jose Owen Vargara Salazar;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JOSE VARGARA SALAZAR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to vacate the January 9, 2010 judgment based on the Oct. 30, 2019 arbitration award is granted.

Under Cal.Rules of Court Rule 3.826, "[w]ithin 60 days after the arbitration award is filed with the clerk of the court, a party may request a trial by filing with the clerk a request for trial." Courts do not require strict compliance with the rule, however. "[S]ince the purposes and policies of the laws may be satisfied by substantial compliance, we adopt that test here." (Hanf v. Sunnyview Development, Inc. (1982) 128 Cal.App.3d 909, 916 [holding that party who filed a "rejection of arbitrator's award" within the time limit, but did not file a request for trial until later, "substantially complied" with the rules to request a new trial].) Here, too, Defendant substantially complied. Defendant attempted to file a request for trial de novo on November 25, 2019, within 60 days of the award entered on October 30. (Terrelle Decl. ISO Mot., 2.) While Defendant's filing was rejected because of a clerical issue, it was served on Plaintiff and was sufficient to apprise Plaintiff and the Court of Defendant's intention to seek a trial de novo. (See id. 4.) "We thus conclude that the rejection substantially complied with the rules governing a request for a trial after arbitration, making the trial court's entry of judgment erroneous." (Hanf, 128 Cal.App.3d at 916.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
